_notes.timezone: UTC. All gating is done in UTC so US and UK are handled by one wraparound window instead of a single local clock. post_thread.py is DST-aware via zoneinfo, so if you later switch to a named tz (e.g. America/New_York) the offset tracks daylight saving automatically.
_notes.active_hours: 13:00-05:00 UTC (crosses midnight). Covers UK afternoon/evening (13-22 UTC) through US Eastern + Pacific prime (23-05 UTC). This is a wide dual-region band, NOT tuned to data yet. After ~2 weeks, read hour_scores in performance.json and narrow to your real best UTC hours.
_notes.posts_per_day: 6, up from 4, because the window is ~16h wide and spans two regions - you need more posts so something lands in each region's prime slot. Still conservative; ramp up only if engagement holds and you see no spam friction.
_notes.income_proof: Present in the list but AUTO-DISABLED at runtime unless products.json.product.real_metrics is set with REAL figures. Never add fake numbers to enable it.
_notes.cta_guidance: Analyzed accounts kept raw product links out of the post body; CTAs were bio-based or pointed to a free resource. The 0.45 include_cta rate is reasonable; consider lowering it.
